status: PRIMARY ABSTRACT DIRECTLY EXTRACTED — FULL TEXT PARTIALLY ACCESS-RESTRICTED

primary_source.title: Therapeutic potential of pro-angiogenic BPC157 is associated with VEGFR2 activation and up-regulation

key_findings: The abstract reports increased vessel density in CAM and endothelial tube-formation assays.
The abstract reports accelerated blood-flow recovery and increased vessel number in the rat hind-limb ischemia model.
BPC-157 increased VEGFR2 mRNA and protein expression in human vascular endothelial cells in vitro, but not VEGF-A.
The study reports VEGFR2 internalization and activation of the VEGFR2-Akt-eNOS pathway.
limitations: Human cells in vitro are not equivalent to treatment of humans.
Full article content was subscription-restricted during Build 0010.
Pro-angiogenic signalling is a mechanistic finding and does not prove either clinical benefit or causal cancer risk.
